name: DevinStorage
description: Devin Operations & ADO Best Practices — Knowledge, Playbooks, Scripts, and Analysis History

repo_notes.content: 5-session pipeline processes ADO work items: Session 0 (gate, <=1 ACU) -> Session D (triage, <=3 ACU) -> Session A (code analysis, <=5 ACU) -> Session B (Wiki documentation, <=3 ACU) -> Session C (test coverage, <=5 ACU). Each session produces artifacts for the next. The only trigger tag is devin-process.

Machine-facing data stored as JSON in analyses/{product}/{slug}.json. Human-facing documentation stored as markdown in ADO Wiki pages at /Functionalities/{slug}. Both updated by the session pipeline and kept in sync.

4 separate PATs with minimum scope: ADO_PAT_WORKITEMS (Work Items R&W), ADO_PAT_WIKI (Wiki R&W), ADO_PAT_CODE (Code Read), ADO_PAT_TESTS (Test Mgmt R&W). No hardcoded values anywhere. All credentials from Devin Secrets Manager.

All ADO scripts pin to api-version=7.1. No preview versions in production (except comments API at 7.1-preview.4). Content-Type for work item operations must be application/json-patch+json. Field names are case-sensitive. Wiki PUT updates require ETag from prior GET.

When any ADO API call fails, check docs/error-catalog.md FIRST. Never search online for ADO API troubleshooting. New errors are added to the catalog after resolution, building institutional memory over time.

All ADO operations use hardened scripts in scripts/ado/. Playbooks reference scripts, never raw curl commands. Scripts handle auth headers, Content-Types, URL encoding, and error checking consistently.

Session A enforces hard stops: max 5 models, max 10 entry points, trace one level deep only. When limits are hit, Devin posts a clarification comment and exits cleanly rather than burning ACUs on unbounded exploration.
